name: Phased Array
description: A phased array antenna consists of multiple radiating elements with controllable phase relationships to electronically steer the beam direction without physical movement. The array can achieve high gain, narrow beamwidth, and rapid beam scanning through precise phase control of individual elements.

num_elements.name: Number of Elements
num_elements.description: Total number of radiating elements in the array

scan_angle.name: Scan Angle
scan_angle.description: Maximum beam steering angle from broadside

phase_shift.name: Progressive Phase Shift
phase_shift.description: Phase difference between adjacent elements for beam steering

array_length.name: Array Length
array_length.description: Physical length of the linear array

taper_coefficient.name: Amplitude Taper Coefficient
taper_coefficient.description: Amplitude weighting coefficient for sidelobe control

designMethodology.overview: Phased array design involves determining element type, spacing, number of elements, and feed network configuration to meet gain, beamwidth, and scanning requirements while maintaining acceptable impedance matching and sidelobe levels.

steps.title: Determine Array Requirements
steps.description: Specify gain, beamwidth, scan range, and frequency band requirements

steps.title: Calculate Number of Elements
steps.description: Estimate required number of elements based on gain and aperture size

steps.title: Set Element Spacing
steps.description: Choose spacing to avoid grating lobes within scan range

steps.title: Design Array Geometry
steps.description: Arrange elements in linear, planar, or conformal configuration

steps.title: Calculate Phase Shifts
steps.description: Determine progressive phase shift for desired beam direction

steps.title: Apply Amplitude Tapering
steps.description: Design amplitude distribution to control sidelobe levels

steps.title: Design Feed Network
steps.description: Create corporate or series feed network with required phase shifts

steps.title: Analyze Mutual Coupling
steps.description: Account for coupling effects between adjacent elements

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use active impedance matrix including mutual coupling between elements with Z_mn = R_mn + j*X_mn
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Multiply element pattern by array factor: E_total(theta,phi) = E_element(theta,phi) * AF(theta,phi)

references: Balanis "Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design" Chapter 6
Stutzman & Thiele "Antenna Theory and Design" Chapter 11
